seo.title: Building Research Agents with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Research Agents with OpenAI's Agents SDK enables multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis workflows for reliable information retrieval. Guardrails are essential to prevent handoff loops, validate tool outputs, and ensure claim accuracy in production systems. Vex integrates seamlessly as output guardrails to mitigate common failures and verify research quality.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically feature a Supervisor Agent that orchestrates specialized sub-agents: Search Agent (web queries), Analysis Agent (fact extraction), and Synthesis Agent (summary generation). Handoffs occur via the SDK's agent.run() with context passing, enabling iterative refinement. Tracing spans multi-agent chains for debugging.

The workflow starts with user queries routed to Search Agent using tools like Tavily or Serper, then passes raw results to Analysis Agent for claim extraction and source verification. Finally, Synthesis Agent compiles verified insights with recency and bias checks. Vex guardrails wrap each agent's output to validate before handoff.

This modular design scales for complex research but introduces risks like unvalidated search results propagating errors or infinite handoff loops between verification agents.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    ↓
[Supervisor Agent]
    ├──→ [Search Agent] ──(raw results)──→
    ├──→ [Analysis Agent] ──(claims+sources)──→
    └──→ [Synthesis Agent] ──(final report)──→
         ↑ Vex Guardrails on each handoff
         Tracing spans entire workflow

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Supervisor endlessly routes between Search and Analysis agents due to incomplete context or vague handoff instructions
risks.mitigation: Vex loop detection guardrail with max iterations and cycle breaking

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious queries trick research agents into bypassing source verification or returning unvalidated claims
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and output validation independent of agent prompts

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Search tool returns outdated/irrelevant results passed directly to synthesis without recency or relevance checks
risks.mitigation: Vex data quality checks on tool outputs before agent processing

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Partial traces lose context during agent handoffs, making debugging synthesis errors impossible
risks.mitigation: Vex unified tracing spans across all agent boundaries

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Supervisor and specialized agents with handoff instructions using OpenAI Agents SDK
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

search_agent = Agent(
    name="Search Agent",
    instructions="Find authoritative sources for research queries using web search tools",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=["tavily_search"]
)

analysis_agent = Agent(
    name="Analysis Agent",
    instructions="Extract claims, verify sources, check recency and bias from search results",
    model="gpt-4o"
)

supervisor = Agent(
    name="Research Supervisor",
    instructions="Orchestrate search → analysis → synthesis workflow",
    sub_agents=[search_agent, analysis_agent]
)

runner = Runner(client)

implementation.title: Multi-Step Research Workflow
implementation.description: Execute orchestrated workflow with context passing between agents
implementation.code:
async def run_research(query: str):
    result = await runner.run(
        supervisor,
        "Research: " + query,
        context={"max_iterations": 5}
    )
    
    # Extract final synthesis
    synthesis = result.final_output
    return {
        "query": query,
        "sources": result.artifacts.get('sources', []),
        "summary": synthesis
    }

# Usage
research_result = await run_research("Latest AI safety research")

implementation.title: Search Tool Integration
implementation.description: Configure Tavily search tool for web research with result limits

implementation.title: Handoff Context Management
implementation.description: Pass verified sources between agents while maintaining trace context
implementation.code:
async def orchestrated_research(query: str):
    # Step 1: Search
    search_results = await runner.run(search_agent, query)
    
    # Step 2: Analysis with search context
    analysis_input = f"Analyze these results: {search_results.output}"
    verified_claims = await runner.run(analysis_agent, analysis_input)
    
    return verified_claims.output

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap each agent output with Vex verification for research quality
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail, GuardrailConfig

# Configure research-specific guardrails
research_guardrail = VexGuardrail(
    config=GuardrailConfig(
        risk_profile="medium",
        verifications=[
            "source_verification",
            "claim_accuracy",
            "recency_checks",
            "bias_detection"
        ],
        max_iterations=5
    )
)

async def guarded_agent_run(agent, input):
    raw_output = await runner.run(agent, input)
    verified_output = await research_guardrail.verify(raw_output.final_output)
    return verified_output

vexIntegration.title: Loop Prevention Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Prevent infinite handoffs with Vex cycle detection
vexIntegration.code:
async def safe_handoff(supervisor, query, context={}):
    context["vex_session"] = "research-" + str(uuid.uuid4())
    
    result = await runner.run(
        supervisor,
        query,
        context=context,
        tools=[research_guardrail.as_tool()]
    )
    
    if research_guardrail.detects_loop(result):
        return "Research terminated: excessive iterations detected"
    
    return result.final_output

vexIntegration.title: Source Verification Pipeline
vexIntegration.description: Vex validates search results before analysis agent consumption
vexIntegration.code:
async def verify_search_results(search_output):
    verification_result = await research_guardrail.verify(
        search_output,
        checks=[
            "source_reputation",  # Domain authority
            "recency_30d",        # Published within 30 days
            "content_quality"     # No obvious spam
        ]
    )
    
    if not verification_result.passed:
        raise ValueError(f"Search failed verification: {verification_result.issues}")
    
    return search_output

# Integrate in workflow
verified_sources = await verify_search_results(search_results)
